metric_value: Gateway's all-in-one PC (circa 2003) exhibits nice integration and cable management.
Intel P4 Extreme Edition specification: 3.2 GHz, 2 MB L3 cache, 'gamer processor' positioning, 30-60 day availability.
90nm Prescott P4 publicly demoed with PCI Express graphics at the IDF; Q4 2003 ship commitment.
Balanced Technology Extended (BTX) positioned as quiet, small form factor ('Bigwater'), instant on-off, resilient power.
notes: [UNVERIFIED] actual-outcome paired — BTX commercially failed by 2007.
BTX adoption was minimal; ATX remained dominant; Intel withdrew BTX support around 2006-2007.

metric_value: iCube (Korean) digital media adapter streams from Sony Vaio notebook to TV, using on-the-fly MPEG-2 → MPEG-4 compression.
notes: Early example of real-time transcoded home media streaming.

metric_value: DTCP-over-IP uses PKI-based authentication to unlock, unscramble, and decompress protected home-network content.
notes: DTCP-IP became standard for DLNA-protected content.
